18	// execute-bead's worker and the post-merge reviewer. It resolves how the
19	// agent invocation is executed (test-injected runner, pre-built service, or

26	//     applying any AgentRunRuntime overrides.

28	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
29	//     and dispatch via executeOnService.

32	// PermissionsOverride, SessionLogDirOverride) take precedence over the
33	// matching rcfg accessors when non-empty, so callers can pin one knob for
34	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
35	func dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, svc agentlib.DdxAgent, runner AgentRunner, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {

input.content:
# Adaptive Min-Tier Routing

`ddx agent execute-loop` automatically promotes the minimum-tier floor from
`cheap` to `standard` when the trailing-window cheap-tier success rate falls
below a fixed threshold. The mechanism saves the queue from burning time
and budget on a tier whose attempts almost never succeed.

This document describes:

1. How the mechanism works
2. Where the metric state lives on disk and what its schema is
3. The reset workflow and how it differs from `--no-adaptive-min-tier`
4. The diagnostic surface you read to debug a "why is cheap being skipped"
   scenario

## How it works

When `execute-loop` starts a draining pass it consults the trailing
window of recent execution outcomes:

- The window is the most recent `N` entries under
  `.ddx/executions/<timestamp>/result.json` (default `N = 50`, set via
  `--adaptive-min-tier-window`).
- Each entry's `(harness, model)` is reverse-resolved through
  `agent.ResolveModelTier` to the tier it was sampled at. Entries whose
  model does not match any tier (e.g. an ad-hoc `--model` pin) are
  ignored.
- An entry is a **counted attempt** when its `failure_mode` is *not* in
  the excluded set (see [What is excluded](#what-is-excluded)).
- The cheap-tier success rate = `successes / attempts`, where
  `successes` is the count of entries whose `outcome` is
  `task_succeeded`.

The cheap tier is suppressed for the upcoming pass when:

- `attempts >= AdaptiveMinTierMinSamples` (currently 3), AND
- `success_rate < AdaptiveMinTierThreshold` (currently 0.20).

When suppressed, the loop emits a single line on its own stdout:

```
adaptive min-tier: skipping cheap tier (trailing success rate 0.10 over 10 attempts; threshold 0.20) — min-tier=standard
```

and the per-bead escalation logic starts at `standard` instead of `cheap`
for the rest of the pass.

### What is excluded

Not every cheap-tier failure should count. A model that never ran cannot
be blamed for failing. The escalation package excludes attempts whose
`failure_mode` matches a routing/configuration class:

| Failure mode    | When set                                                        |
|-----------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| `routing_error` | `prepareArtifacts` failed (context build), or dispatch returned an error before any agent result was produced (no API key, no viable provider, model not in catalog). |

`routing_error` attempts are still written to `result.json` and remain
visible in `route-status`, but they do not contribute to the trailing
success rate. This prevents a configuration regression — for example a
mis-pinned model in `agent.routing.model_overrides` — from condemning
cheap tier even after the configuration is fixed.

The set is enforced in `escalation.AdaptiveExcludedFailureModes`. The
agent package keeps the canonical mode names; a guard test
(`agent.TestRoutingFailureModeMatchesEscalationVocab`) catches drift.

## Where the state lives

Two on-disk locations matter:

| Path                                                | Purpose                                        |
|-----------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|
| `.ddx/executions/<ts>/result.json`                  | Raw input. One file per attempt.               |
| `.ddx/agent-state/adaptive-min-tier.json`           | Reset checkpoint (created on first reset).     |

`AdaptiveMinTier` reads `result.json` files only. The
`routing-outcomes.jsonl` file under `.ddx/agent-logs/` is consulted by
`ddx agent route-status` for routing-decision *display*, not by the
adaptive metric.

### `result.json` schema (subset relevant to adaptive)

```json
{
  "harness": "claude",
  "model": "claude-haiku-4-5",
  "outcome": "task_failed",
  "failure_mode": "routing_error"
}
```

`outcome` enum: `task_succeeded`, `task_failed`, `task_no_changes`.
`failure_mode` is empty on success and one of the
`agent.FailureMode*` constants otherwise.

### `adaptive-min-tier.json` schema

```json
{
  "reset_at": "20260429T132000-zzzzzzzz",
  "reset_by": "ddx agent route-status reset",
  "reset_reason": "fixed model_overrides typo"
}
```

`reset_at` is a lexicographic high-water mark. `AdaptiveMinTier`
excludes any execution directory whose name sorts at or below this value
from the trailing window. Execution directories use the
`YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS-<hash>` naming convention; the `-zzzzzzzz` suffix on the
checkpoint guarantees same-second collisions sort below the checkpoint.

## Reset workflow

When the cheap tier is wedged in a `skipped` state because of a
configuration bug that has since been fixed, run:

```sh
ddx agent route-status reset --yes
```

Output (text mode):

```
adaptive min-tier reset:
  state file written: /path/to/project/.ddx/agent-state/adaptive-min-tier.json
  reset_at:           20260429T132000-zzzzzzzz
  scope:              all
  not touched:        /path/to/project/.ddx/executions
                      /path/to/project/.ddx/agent-logs/routing-outcomes.jsonl
next execute-loop run will re-evaluate cheap tier on its own merits.
```

Without `--yes`, the command prints a confirm prompt and exits without
changing anything — re-run with `--yes` to apply.

`--reason "<text>"` is optional and recorded into the state file's
`reset_reason` field for audit.

`--scope` accepts `cheap` or `all`. Today both share the same checkpoint
because the trailing window is computed across all tiers in one pass; the
flag is preserved for forward-compatibility with a per-tier reset.

`--json` emits a machine-readable record naming every file/store written
and every store explicitly NOT touched.

### Reset vs `--no-adaptive-min-tier`

These two knobs solve different problems:

| Knob                                  | Effect                                    | When to use                        |
|---------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|------------------------------------|
| `execute-loop --no-adaptive-min-tier` | Bypass the gate for one run only.          | One-off override; permanent state untouched. |
| `route-status reset`                  | Clear the trailing window so the gate re-evaluates from scratch. | Configuration bug is fixed; re-establish a clean baseline. |

If you find yourself reaching for `--no-adaptive-min-tier` repeatedly,
the right move is `route-status reset` once.

## Diagnostic surface

`ddx agent route-status` emits an `Adaptive Min-Tier Status` section that
shows everything needed to debug a `cheap-skipped` scenario without
reading source:

```
Adaptive Min-Tier Status
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Window:        50
Threshold:     0.20 cheap-tier success rate (min 3 samples)
Effective floor: standard  (cheap tier currently SKIPPED)
State file:    /path/to/.ddx/agent-state/adaptive-min-tier.json
TIER       ATTEMPTS  SUCCESSES  SUCCESS_RATE  EXCLUDED_ROUTING
cheap      9         0          0.00          0
standard   0         0          0.00          0
smart      0         0          0.00          0
Reset:         ddx agent route-status reset --yes
```

The same data is available as JSON via `route-status --json` under the
top-level `adaptive_min_tier` key. JSON callers can inspect:

- `window`, `threshold`, `min_samples`, `effective_floor`, `cheap_skipped`
- `reset_at` (empty when no reset has been recorded)
- `state_file` (the absolute path to `adaptive-min-tier.json`)
- `excluded_routing_failures` (cheap-tier attempts skipped because of
  `routing_error` classification)
- `tiers["cheap" | "standard" | "smart"]` with per-tier
  `attempts`, `successes`, `success_rate`,
  `excluded_routing_failures`.

## Tuning

Defaults are conservative on purpose: a 50-entry window at a 20%
threshold needs at least 3 samples and ensures one bad day does not
permanently blacklist cheap tier. Override the window via
`--adaptive-min-tier-window <N>` on `execute-loop`; the threshold and
min-sample constants are not currently runtime-configurable.
